mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% signals an extended period of monetary accommodation. This ultra-low rate environment reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 1,702 thousand units represent a contraction from prior cycle peaks, signaling weakening residential demand. This demand deterioration precedes broader economic slowdown and equity market correction within 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 15% over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where HOUST declines exceed 15% over 3 months, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over the test period.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 149,629 thousand combined with unemployment rate at 4.0% indicates a tight labor market near full employment. This labor tightness constrains profit margins through wage pressure and reduces equity valuations, producing negative 20-day returns as investors price in margin compression.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.5% and PAYEMS growth positive
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE shows Rank IC < 0.01 when tested as an interaction term over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 101.89 (index 2017=100) reflects near-trend manufacturing capacity utilization. A sustained decline in INDPRO below 100 signals supply-side weakness and deflationary pressure, which increases real discount rates and produces negative equity returns over 5 trading days as growth expectations reset downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 100.5 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of periods when INDPRO is below 100.5, or the signal shows Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 626,833 million dollars combined with housing starts at 1,702 thousand units reveal synchronized weakness in consumer and residential demand. This dual demand contraction triggers a 60-day equity market correction as forward GDP growth expectations decline and earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth < 2% annualized and HOUST < 1,600 thousand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 281.93 (index 1982-1984=100) combined with federal funds rate at 0.08% indicates the Fed has moved into an easing stance relative to inflation. This monetary accommodation relative to price pressures supports equity valuations and produces positive 20-day returns as real rates decline.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines 0.25pp or more in prior month while CPIAUCSL growth remains below 3% annualized
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction of rate cuts and inflation shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.0% represents a cyclical low point in the labor market. At this stage of the cycle, labor market tightness has peaked and the next directional move is typically upward, signaling the onset of economic deceleration that produces negative 60-day equity returns as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE at or below 4.0% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE is at or below 4.0%, or the signal shows Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 19,805.96 billion chained 2017 dollars combined with retail sales and housing starts shows demand-side momentum. A sustained acceleration in GDPC1 growth above 2.5% annualized, confirmed by rising RSAFS and HOUST, produces positive 20-day equity returns as earnings growth expectations increase.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized and RSAFS growth > 3% annualized
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction of GDP and retail sales shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.
